commit.message:
Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs`
  - Both sections explicitly state:
    - plugin specs and Agent Skills are complementary, not replacements
- skillpm’s model is unchanged (`npm` for deps + `skills` for wiring +
`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- **Docs consistency**
- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

Example excerpt added:

```md
- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.
```

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(reconcile): use MultiPartitionKey and sanitize tag values

Two bugs surfaced when running the reconciliation script in production:

1. Tag value validation error: Dagster only allows [a-zA-Z0-9_-.] in tag
   values (max 63 chars).  The 'dagster/reconciled_from_partition' tag was
   set to the raw partition key string (e.g. MITx-15.415x-3T2018-course|edge)
   which contains '|', an invalid character.  Fix: added _tag_safe() helper
   that replaces '|' with '__' and truncates to 63 chars.

2. Partition key type: course_and_source is a DynamicPartitionsDefinition
   whose keys are MultiPartitionKey objects (course_id + source_system
   dimensions).  The script was building plain strings for add_dynamic_partitions
   and AssetMaterialization, which may not round-trip correctly.  Fix:
   - Replaced _build_partition_key() with _make_multi_partition_key() that
     returns a proper MultiPartitionKey({'course_id': ..., 'source_system': ...})
   - Use str(mk) for existence checks against get_dynamic_partitions() results
   - Import MultiPartitionKey from dagster

Also added '# ruff: noqa: INP001' since the script lives in dg_deployments/
which has no __init__.py (consistent with how bin/ scripts suppress this rule).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

* fix(pdf-ocr): always include text instruction in OCR API calls

The OpenRouter docs show that ALL examples require a text instruction
alongside the file item. Omitting it for mistral-ocr-latest caused the
model to return empty content. Now all models always get the instruction
placed before the file item, matching the documented API format."

Co-authored-by: merlinbecker <3381241+merlinbecker@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(pdf-ocr): make model required, remove non-existent mistral-ocr-latest default

- Remove DEFAULT_OCR_MODEL constant (mistral-ocr-latest does not exist on OpenRouter)
- Make `model` a required parameter with an error returned when omitted
- Remove isDedicatedOcrModel branching — single unified empty-content error hint
  that includes the actual model name and links to openrouter.ai/models
- Update factory description: model is now required
- Update all tests to pass explicit model; add test for missing model validation

Co-authored-by: merlinbecker <3381241+merlinbecker@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(tests): merge duplicate OCR engine/instruction test blocks into it.each

The two `it("uses mistral-ocr engine...")` tests were nearly identical (41
duplicated lines across 2 blocks, flagged by SonarQube). Since the
isDedicatedOcrModel distinction was removed, they tested the same code
path with different model strings. Merged into a single it.each that
parameterises over both models — same coverage, zero duplication.

feat: Stadtsuche mit automatischer Koordinatenübernahme (Nominatim API)

Neue Funktion: Der Benutzer kann im Hauptfenster einen Stadtnamen
eingeben und auf 'Suchen' klicken. Die Koordinaten werden dann
automatisch aus der OpenStreetMap Nominatim API übernommen.

Neue Dateien:
  - Models/GeocodingResult.cs:
    Datenmodell für Nominatim-API-Response (jsonv2); enthält lat/lon
    als Strings und berechnet double-Properties mit InvariantCulture.
    ShortName gibt den ersten Teil des DisplayName zurück.

  - Services/GeocodingService.cs:
    REST-Client für Nominatim API mit korrektem User-Agent-Header,
    Accept-Language: de sowie featuretype=settlement-Filter.
    Ergebnisse werden nach Importance absteigend sortiert.

  - Forms/CitySelectionForm.cs + .Designer.cs:
    Modaler Auswahldialog für mehrere Treffer. Zeigt Treffer in
    einer ListBox; bei Auswahl werden Koordinaten + Typ angezeigt.
    Doppelklick bestätigt sofort. Vollständig im App-Farbschema.

    txtLocationName etwas schmaler (155px statt 200px).

and resume updating DNS records.

fix: auto-update now detects prerelease versions (alpha/beta/rc)

Three root causes fixed:
- Changed from /releases/latest to /releases API — the /latest endpoint
  silently skips prerelease-tagged releases, so beta/rc versions were
  never found
- Added proper pre-release version comparison (alpha < beta < rc < release)
  so 1.0.0 is correctly detected as newer than 1.0.0-beta
- Release workflow now auto-detects prerelease tags (-alpha/-beta/-rc)
  and marks the GitHub Release accordingly

Also added comprehensive debug logging to the update check flow so
failures are diagnosable from the app log.

refactor: split NAPI Browser impl (6,951→1,047 lines, 85% reduction)

Extract 8 domain modules from the monolithic NativeBrowser impl block:
- browser_agent.rs (976): agent, vision, memory, emulation, safety
- browser_automation.rs (1,041): scheduler, sessions, events, workflow
- browser_crawl.rs (940): spider, stealth, anti-bot, captcha, geofencing
- browser_dom.rs (897): selectors, DOM, accessibility, storage, coverage
- browser_mobile.rs (706): iOS + Android automation
- browser_network.rs (651): HAR, throttling, TLS, HTTP client, proxy
- browser_media.rs (451): screenshots, PDF, recording, VRT
- browser_input.rs (308): cookies, keyboard, forms, human simulation

NAPI-RS supports multiple #[napi] impl blocks across files.
Zero warnings, all tests pass.

Add three new informational pages for Sud-Kivu province website

  national park's biodiversity (gorilles de Grauer, fauna/flora),
  conservation challenges, tourism activities, and government engagement

- lac-kivu-tanganyika.html: Page covering both major lakes — Lac Kivu
  (methane reserves, sambaza fishing, Idjwi island) and Lac Tanganyika
  (deepest lake in Africa, endemic fish species, cross-border commerce),
  plus hydroelectric and tourism potential

- ressources-minieres.html: Mining resources page covering gold, coltan,
  cassiterite and wolframite deposits across Mwenga, Shabunda, Fizi and
  Kalehe territories, the provincial quinquennial strategy including
  anti-fraud brigades, formalization of artisanal cooperatives, and
  responsible governance framework

All three pages use the exact same HTML structure, CSS, header/navigation
and footer as territoire-fizi.html, with only the title, meta description
and main content customized for each topic.

  UsbManager.requestPermission() showing the system Allow dialog, stores
  result in sPermissionGranted. Uses RECEIVER_NOT_EXPORTED on API 33+.
- Add hasPermission(ctx): checks UsbManager directly.
- Add isPermissionGranted(): C++ polls this flag.

  as Q_INVOKABLEs calling the Java statics above.

  and starts a 500ms poll Timer.
- Waiting banner shown while permission dialog is pending.
- Connect button disabled until hasUsbPermission() == true.

commit.message:
Multi-selection v1: Ctrl+click additive select, group operations

State model: STATE.selectedId (number|null)  STATE.selectedIds (Set)
with primarySelectedId() helper for workbench context.

Selection: Ctrl/Cmd+click toggles elements in/out of selection.
Plain click replaces selection. Ctrl+A selects all.

Group operations: delete, flip H/V, duplicate, lock/unlock, and
layer reorder all operate on the full selection set. Lock toggles
use any-unlocked heuristic (if any unlocked  lock all, else unlock).
Duplicate clones all selected with offset, selects all clones.
Arrow nudge moves all selected elements together.

Overlay: single selection shows full handles (resize/rotate/edge).
Multi-selection shows per-element dashed bounding boxes without
handles  move only, no resize/rotate.

Workbenches: carve and ink panels show 'N elements selected' message
when multi-selected. Single selection retains full editing. Carve
focus requires single selection.

Group drag: all selected unlocked elements move together with the
existing 6px dead zone. Undo captures all element positions.
